PROJECTS

Things I've built

A mix of production systems with real users, and things I built because I was curious if they'd work.

Gesture-Controlled PC Interface
Featured · Personal

A real-time hand-tracking system that replaces the mouse entirely — move your index finger to move the cursor, pinch to click, hold the pinch to drag, and make a fist to instantly pause control. Built with MediaPipe's 21-point hand landmark model, tuned with a moving-average smoother and a deadzone filter to kill hand-shake jitter, and a pinch threshold that scales with hand size so it works at any distance from the camera.

[ Python ][ OpenCV ][ MediaPipe ][ PyAutoGUI ]
idle

Click "Try Live Demo" to run real hand-tracking in your browser. Nothing leaves your device.

Index finger — cursor position
Thumb + index pinch — click / drag
Fist — pause / resume control
AI SEO SaaS Platform
Production

Full-stack AI-powered SEO analysis platform with 100+ paying users. Real-time audits of metadata, headings, content structure and technical SEO factors, plus a lead-gen flow that converts site traffic into qualified leads.

ABOUT

Backend instincts.
Real production systems.

I'm a backend-focused software developer at Pixel Global, where I design APIs, build data pipelines, and ship AI-powered systems that real businesses depend on — not demos, production.

I've built systems processing 500+ records per run, APIs serving 100+ paying users, and automation pipelines that cut manual work by ~90%. I care about things working reliably, not just in a demo.

Outside client work, I build things I'm curious about — like controlling a full desktop cursor using nothing but hand landmarks from a webcam.
